title: The total change of a conservative update is the negative scaled boundary flux difference

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a conservative update, the updated total minus the original total equals negative scale times the boundary flux difference flux(n)-flux(0).
assumptions: u and flux are real-valued functions on natural-number grid indices
totalOn sums a state over Finset.range n
informal.expected_conclusion: The total change is exactly the negative scaled boundary flux difference.

declarations.name: totalOn_conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (flux n - flux 0)

declarations.name: totalChange_conservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalChange_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n - totalOn u n = -scale * (flux n - flux 0)

tasks.input: Translate the total-change identity for a conservative update into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem totalChange_conservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u flux : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => conservativeUpdate scale u flux i) n - totalOn u n = -scale * (flux n - flux 0)

tasks.input: Given totalOn_conservativeUpdate, complete the Lean 4 proof of the total-change identity.
tasks.target: A proof by rewriting with totalOn_conservativeUpdate and closing with ring.
